phases.name: Corrective column rename + estimated-portion recorder (backend forward recording)
phases.description: Rename the misnamed Slice-1 column EstimatedTotalWork to EstimatedItemCount end-to-end (it never held data) via a corrective migration, then forward-record the estimated portion of each day's backlog — the extrapolated work contributed by not-yet-broken-down features — onto the same DeliveryMetricSnapshot row, on the same PortfolioForecastsUpdated handler. The backlog total already includes the inferred estimate (WorkItemService extrapolation); this records how much of it is estimated.

steps.name: Corrective rename EstimatedTotalWork -> EstimatedItemCount across model, DTO, FE model, and a migration on real providers
criteria: DeliveryMetricSnapshot.EstimatedTotalWork is renamed to EstimatedItemCount (nullable int) and the LighthouseAppContext mapping follows
A rename migration is generated for Sqlite and Postgres and applies cleanly on a real (non-InMemory) provider with no PendingModelChangesWarning at startup
DeliveryMetricsHistoryPointDto.EstimatedTotalWork and the FE DeliveryMetricsHistoryPoint.estimatedTotalWork are renamed to estimatedItemCount, with the boundary parser key updated
The existing backend + frontend suites stay green after the rename (behaviour-preserving refactor); the read-API fixture and the recorder-test stub reference the new name
steps.scenario_name: Refactor verification: the existing every-series read-API facet stays green keyed on the renamed estimatedItemCount field
steps.implementation_notes: REFACTOR step (behaviour-preserving) — NO new Gherkin scenario is added; verification is three-fold: (a) the existing BodyCarriesEverySeries facet in the read-API integration test, adjusted to key on the renamed estimatedItemCount field, stays green; (b) the rename migration applies on a real Sqlite + Postgres provider with no PendingModelChangesWarning; (c) the full backend + frontend suites stay green. Keep this commit separate from the 01-02 feature commit (workflow.instructions refactor-separation). The Slice-1 column EstimatedTotalWork was provisioned under a now-corrected premise (see feature-delta ## Changed Assumptions) and has ALWAYS been written null, so renaming is safe. Rename the model property, the EF mapping, the DTO record field (DeliveryMetricsHistoryPointDto), the FE interface field + the parsePoint key in DeliveryMetricsHistory.ts (asNullableNumber(point.estimatedItemCount, ...)). Generate the migration ONLY via Lighthouse.Backend/Create-Migration.ps1 -MigrationName RenameEstimatedTotalWorkToEstimatedItemCount (does sqlite + throwaway-postgres); NEVER dotnet ef migrations add. EF MIGRATION TRAP: a model property rename needs a real migration or real sqlite/postgres verify jobs FATAL on PendingModelChangesWarning at startup while InMemory stays green — the applies-on-real-provider check is mandatory. UPDATE the existing references so the suite compiles & stays green: the read-API fixture SeedDeliveryWithEverySeriesRecorded (EstimatedTotalWork = 15) + BodyCarriesEverySeries + HistoryPointView record, and the recorder-test stub assertion. The 'response carries the renamed field' scenario is satisfied by the existing BodyCarriesEverySeries facet now keyed on estimatedItemCount — adjust it, do not add a new test. nullable enabled, no dynamic. No decorative/structural/provenance comments.

steps.name: Recorder forward-records the estimated portion (extrapolated work) into EstimatedItemCount
criteria: The recorder sets EstimatedItemCount = the sum of FeatureWork.TotalWorkItems over the delivery's features where IsUsingDefaultFeatureSize is true (the extrapolated portion of the backlog total) — milestone-2 scenario 1, the RED driver

FOLDED milestone-2 scenario 2 (triangulation): a fully broken-down delivery (no IsUsingDefaultFeatureSize features) records EstimatedItemCount == null. This is the null branch of the same ternary as scenario 1 — it goes green the moment the ternary is written, so it cannot be an independent failing test and is verified by a sibling assert in this step, NOT a separate step
Re-handling the same event the same day overwrites EstimatedItemCount in place (idempotent on deliveryId and date), never duplicating rows
The recorded estimated portion never exceeds the recorded TotalWork (it is a subset of the backlog total already counted in Slice 1)

The [Ignore] is dropped from the HandleAsync_NotBrokenDownFeature_* recorder test and the throwing stub fixture SeedDeliveryWithNotBrokenDownFeature (test line 221) is implemented as a delivery with an IsUsingDefaultFeatureSize feature whose FeatureWork sums to a known size
steps.scenario_name: A not-yet-broken-down feature's extrapolated items are recorded as the estimated portion
steps.implementation_notes: RE-USE and reframe the pre-scaffolded [Ignore] stub HandleAsync_NotBrokenDownFeature_RecordsInferredEstimateAboveActualBacklog: rename it (e.g. HandleAsync_NotBrokenDownFeature_RecordsExtrapolatedItemsAsEstimatedPortion), drop the [Ignore], and add the SeedDeliveryWithNotBrokenDownFeature fixture — a delivery with one Feature where IsUsingDefaultFeatureSize=true and FeatureWork rows summing to a known extrapolated size (e.g. 8), simulating the post-extrapolation persisted state the recorder reads in production (WorkItemService.ExtrapolateNotBrokenDownFeatures writes that FeatureWork before forecasting; the recorder runs after). EXTEND HandleAsync: after computing totalWork, compute var estimatedPortion = delivery.Features.Where(f => f.IsUsingDefaultFeatureSize).SelectMany(f => f.FeatureWork).Sum(w => w.TotalWorkItems); set snapshot.EstimatedItemCount = estimatedPortion > 0 ? estimatedPortion : null. CRITICAL: do NOT use Feature.Size (Feature.cs:50 returns 0 for IsUsingDefaultFeatureSize features) — sum FeatureWork.TotalWorkItems directly, matching how totalWork is already computed. Idempotency stays get-or-create overwrite-in-place, NEVER a =true sentinel (forecast-minimum-data-guard lesson). FOLDED SCENARIO (TDD-justified, not an oversight): this step owns milestone-2 scenario 1 (the RED driver, scenario_name) AND scenario 2 'A fully broken-down delivery records no estimated portion' as a sibling assert in the SAME test — scenario 2 is the `: null` branch of the same ternary, so it is green the instant the ternary is written and cannot stand as an independent failing test; splitting it into its own step would create a fake-RED step. Keep the recorder write as `snapshot.EstimatedItemCount = estimatedPortion > 0 ? estimatedPortion : null`. CI: NUnit4002 Is.Null/Is.Zero not Is.EqualTo(null/0); NUnit2056 Assert.EnterMultipleScope with independent asserts (NUnit2045); CA1859 concrete return type on single-impl private members; S3267 prefer LINQ where over manual loop+if; the recorder handler path already cleared S2139 (log-and-swallow, dispatcher swallows per its CA1031 contract) and CA1859 in Slice 1 — keep those shapes; immutable, early returns, max 2 nesting, no comments.

phases.name: Read-API coverage for the estimated portion
phases.description: Assert the renamed estimatedItemCount surfaces through the consolidated metrics-history endpoint, and that a delivery whose snapshots carry only backlog totals returns points with no estimated portion (milestone-2 scenario 3, the before-recorded case). No production code change — the DTO already maps the field after the 01-01 rename.

steps.name: Read-API: before the estimated portion was recorded, the history carries only the backlog total
criteria: A delivery whose snapshots carry only backlog totals (EstimatedItemCount null) returns points that each carry a TotalWork but no estimatedItemCount
The endpoint shape and date ordering are unchanged; only the absence of estimatedItemCount on the historic rows is asserted

The already-recorded estimated portion still surfaces as estimatedItemCount on a populated point (covered by the existing every-series facet from Slice 1, now keyed on the renamed field — not duplicated here)
steps.scenario_name: Before the estimated portion was recorded the history carries only the backlog total
steps.implementation_notes: ADD a facet for milestone-2 scenario 3 (@real-io @error): seed a delivery whose snapshots carry only backlog totals (TotalWork/DoneWork/RemainingWork set, EstimatedItemCount left null), GET metrics-history, assert every point carries a TotalWork and NO point carries an estimatedItemCount. The 'estimated portion surfaces as a field' direction is ALREADY covered by the Slice-1 BodyCarriesEverySeries facet (now keyed estimatedItemCount after 01-01) — do not duplicate it. Reuse the existing HistoryPointView/Points helpers. WebApplicationFactory<Program> + real EF + EnsureDeleted/EnsureCreated. NO production code change (the DTO maps the field after 01-01). CI: NUnit4002; NUnit2056 + NUnit2045; CA1861 hoist any constant array literal to a private static readonly field; S6608 indexer over .First()/.Single() where applicable; factory-function fixtures validated against the production model; no decorative/structural/provenance comments.

phases.name: Estimated-items dotted line + caption (frontend + demo/E2E)
phases.description: Surface how much of the backlog is still estimated on the existing burnup: a dotted 'Estimated (not broken down)' line plotting estimatedItemCount directly — falling toward zero and disappearing as features are broken down, and visually explaining jumps in the Backlog total when estimates are replaced by real items. Prove it end-to-end with a demo-seeded estimated portion and one assertion on the existing walking-skeleton spec.

steps.name: DeliveryBurnupChart plots the dotted estimated-items line (falls to zero) + caption
criteria: When any point carries an estimatedItemCount, the burnup plots a dotted 'Estimated (not broken down)' line of estimatedItemCount directly, alongside the unchanged Backlog and Done lines
The dotted line plots a gap (null) on points whose estimatedItemCount is null or zero, so it falls toward zero and disappears once every feature is broken down
A caption names the latest estimated portion, e.g. '{N} of {M} backlog items are estimated (features not yet broken down)', only when one is present
When no point carries an estimatedItemCount, only the Backlog and Done lines render with no dotted line and no caption (the Slice-1 chart); the forward-only empty state is unchanged
steps.scenario_name: Burnup plots the dotted estimated-items line and the estimated-portion caption
steps.implementation_notes: EXTEND buildSeries: when any point carries estimatedItemCount, append an 'Estimated (not broken down)' line whose data is point.estimatedItemCount mapped to null when it is null OR 0 (so the dotted line gaps and disappears as features break down — the explicit user-requested behaviour). Render it DOTTED (MUI-X series lineStyle strokeDasharray). Do NOT subtract from totalWork — plot the stored estimatedItemCount DIRECTLY; this both trends down over time and visually explains jumps in the Backlog total when an estimate is replaced by real items. Render a Typography caption from the latest point carrying an estimatedItemCount: N = estimatedItemCount, M = totalWork; only when N > 0. NO schema/parser change (the field was renamed in 01-01). RTL test EXTENDS the existing file: renders the dotted 'Estimated (not broken down)' line + caption from a history fixture whose points carry estimatedItemCount and gaps to nothing on a later fully-broken-down point; renders only Backlog+Done with no caption when none carry one. Assert BUSINESS behaviour (the 'Estimated (not broken down)' series + caption text present/absent), not 'renders without crashing'. TS strict, never any (narrow unknown). S107 options-object if a helper crosses 3 params; UPPER_SNAKE_CASE module const for the caption template; no nested ternaries in JSX (extract a helper); Biome clean; no decorative/structural/provenance comments. Mock only the DeliveryService abstraction, never fetch.

steps.name: Demo-seed an estimated portion + extend the walking-skeleton E2E to assert the split
criteria: Demo seeding records an EstimatedItemCount below TotalWork on the demo delivery's snapshots so a fresh demo instance renders the dotted estimated-items line and the caption
The existing DeliveryMetrics walking-skeleton spec asserts the dotted 'Estimated (not broken down)' line / caption is visible alongside backlog and done
The spec runs green against the locally started app with seeded demo data on a premium instance
steps.scenario_name: Forecaster sees the dotted estimated-items line on the demo delivery's burnup
steps.implementation_notes: EXTEND DemoDataService.SeedBurnupSnapshots (DemoDataService.cs:130) to set snapshot.EstimatedItemCount to a deterministic value below TotalWork on the seeded rows (ideally decreasing across the seeded days so the demo shows the dotted line falling), so the demo burnup shows the dotted estimated line + caption. EXTEND the existing @walking_skeleton spec (DeliveryMetrics.spec.ts, the '@walking_skeleton @US-01 forecaster opens a delivery's Metrics tab' case) with ONE assertion that the dotted 'Estimated (not broken down)' line / caption is visible (extend the DeliveryMetricsTab POM with a getter; ALL locators via the POM, never inline page.locator — E2E Page-Object-Model rule). Do NOT add a new spec file — keep E2E minimal (E2E-minimalism feedback); the render logic is covered by the 03-01 RTL test, so the E2E only proves the seam end-to-end. Drive from loadDemoScenario demo data, never live syncs; premium instance via the license-import recipe (reference_premium_license_dev_seed). RUN the spec locally (dotnet run backend + license import + playwright) before committing — pnpm build only type-checks, not locator validity; do not punt to the user. Confirm the demo delivery's snapshot rows are the ones the Metrics tab reads.

validation.notes: Slice-2 roadmap generated 2026-06-02 from slice-02-inferred-estimate.md + milestone-2-inferred-estimate.feature AFTER a code-verified premise correction (feature-delta ## Changed Assumptions): the backlog line already includes extrapolated work (WorkItemService.ExtrapolateNotBrokenDownFeatures), so the slice is reframed from 'a higher inferred line' to 'the estimated portion within the backlog', plotted as a dotted direct-plot line. EstimatedTotalWork (never populated) is renamed to EstimatedItemCount via a corrective migration in 01-01 (refactor, separate commit). Scenario ownership: 01-01 refactor-verification (no Gherkin); 01-02 owns milestone-2 scenario 1 (RED) + scenario 2 folded as triangulation of the same ternary's null branch (cannot be an independent RED); 02-01 owns scenario 3; 03-01/03-02 are FE+E2E. Reviewer returned BLOCK with 6 items; adjudicated: B1/B6 (01-01 scenario_name implied a non-existent scenario) FIXED -> reworded as refactor-verification + notes; B2/B3 (split scenario 2) REJECTED on TDD grounds (null-branch can't be independent RED) and instead made the fold EXPLICIT in criteria+notes; B4 (FE parser path/Zod) DISMISSED as invalid — verified DeliveryMetricsHistory.ts exists with parsePoint and no Zod in the FE; B5 (implement throwing fixture + drop [Ignore]) FIXED -> added as an explicit 01-02 criterion; nits applied (CA1859/S2139 reminder on the recorder path, exact spec named in 03-02).
